content.full_text:
The Law Firm of

Knox, Lemmon & Brady
455 Capitol Mall, Suite 210
Sacramento, California 95814
(916) 448-9911
FAX (916) 448-2566

Louis J. Anapolsky
Matthew V. Brady
Thomas S. Knox
John M. Lemmon
Susan J. Sheridan

OF COUNSEL
Philip M. Knox, Jr.

November 22, 1991

Peter D. Lemmon
Marguerite D. Shea

Ms. Dixie Howard
Fair Political Practices Commission
428 J. Street, Suite 800
Post Office Box 807
Sacramento, CA 95804-0807

Re: CA-NV SUPER SPEED GROUND TRANSPORTATION COMMISSION

Dear Ms. Howard:

This letter follows our conversation yesterday about the need for the Commissioners of
the above-referenced commission to file "Leaving Office Statements" when the commission
technically ends according to the legislation which created it. According to the statute, the
commission expires January 1, 1992. However, as I told you, it appears that legislation may
be signed by the Governor as early as February or March 1992 which would extend the life of
the commission for another year. Please confirm what you told me on the phone; that the
commissioners need only file their yearly reports by April 1, 1992 this year.

I will, as I promised, keep you up to date on address changes for any of the
commissioners.

Very truly yours,

Peter D. Lemmon

cc Chairman Bruce Aguilera
s\sst\fppc.ltr

sections.question_synthetic: When must commissioners of the California Super Speed Ground Transportation Commission file 'Leaving Office Statements' if the commission expires January 1, 1992 but may be extended by legislation signed by February or March 1992?
sections.parsing_notes: No section headers found; LLM: This is an incoming request/inquiry letter from a law firm to FPPC, not an FPPC response letter. No formal QUESTION or CONCLUSION sections are present. The letter references a prior phone conversation and seeks confirmation of deadline information already discussed verbally. Document contains no section headers.

embedding.summary: Law firm Knox, Lemmon & Brady writes to FPPC requesting confirmation of filing deadlines for commissioners' yearly reports, noting the commission technically expires January 1, 1992 but may be extended by pending legislation.
